actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
On AI Agent Memory: i’ve been noticing an interesting hierarchy emerging in AI agent memory systems that almost mirrors how humans form intuition. 

TLDR: I feel like "intuition" in agents is an emergent property of memory plus asynchronous processing over time. Let me explain...

When an LLM pulls data from RAG or from its context window, that’s basically “knowledge” or useful information . The data lives somewhere, you retrieve it when needed, and the model uses it in the moment. That part is well understood.

What starts to feel different is when that knowledge is stored and then revisited. At that point, it’s closer to memory. Especially when it comes from different sources and contexts …things the agent saw on the web, things it experienced in prior interactions, things it inferred over time and stored in a database.

Now the most interesting part: when those memories are processed asynchronously (lot of solutions do this for efficient storage and retrieval of large datasets), not during a live interaction, but offline through reflection, summarization, or consolidation, they start to synthesize. Multiple memories cross-pollinate. Patterns form.   That synthesis is what begins to look like intuition. (Maybe even opinions) 

This maps pretty closely to how humans form long-term memories and experiences too. We’re not doing RAG to recall facts in real time. We try and remember, reflect later, connect experiences across time, and eventually develop a gut sense without consciously replaying everything. (Maybe this is our subconscious mind!). Hello Carl Jung. :) 

A lot of current agent memory work already points in this direction, especially around episodic vs semantic memory and long-term agent behavior:
	•	Episodic Memory is the Missing Piece for Long-Term LLM Agents (arXiv)
https://lnkd.in/gwBV3ZPp
	•	LangChain’s discussion on memory types for agents
https://lnkd.in/g-7DVvBH

We think about a lot of these AI + Psych + Cognitiony things working on machine learning at Headspace.

actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
Happy to share that I just finished Large Language Model Systems course at my alma mater, CMU! 🎉

This course felt like coming full circle. I first took my intro ML course in Fall '19 as a grad student, when transformers were still relatively new, before going on to specialize in systems. Returning to ML a few years later (Fall '25), this time through the systems lens I genuinely enjoy, was incredibly rewarding. Diving into GPUs, memory, distributed training, inference at scale, and what it actually takes to run LLMs in the real world deepened my appreciation for the systems challenges and, sharpened my understanding of the ML side as well.

This wasn’t about calling APIs or lightly fine-tuning black boxes. We built things from the ground up, or went deep into the weeds to understand how a single line of code change could meaningfully change performance.

Over the semester, I worked on 7 substantial mini-projects (it builds up incrementally):

- Building parts of a small educational deep-learning framework (miniTorch) to really understand how autodiff and tensor systems work under the hood
- Writing CUDA kernels for core tensor operations and optimizing them for performance and integrating them with miniTorch
- Implemented and benchmarked a decoder-only transformer (GPT-2) in miniTorch - building core modules like MHA, LayerNorm, and a machine translation pipeline to train and evaluate the model.
- Optimizing transformers on GPU, focusing on memory access patterns and writing fused CUDA kernels
- Scaling training with distributed techniques (data/model parallelism, ZeRO/DeepSpeed, Pipeline parallelism/GPipe)
- Advanced training & inference systems — using DeepSpeed to train and fine-tune large models efficiently (LoRA), and dug into SGLang’s design to tune inference and wring out every bit of performance.
- Building an RLHF system using a VERL-like framework, implementing PPO-based RLHF and reasoning about the design required to make RLHF stable

What I liked the most about this course was how system-oriented everything was. Every design decision came back to the same questions:
Where does the memory go? What’s on GPU vs CPU? Where is the bottleneck? What breaks at scale?

Coming in with a strong systems background, this course gave me a much clearer picture of we actually build, train, and serve LLMs - not just how to use them.

I’m excited to apply this thinking to data and near-data infrastructure where performance, cost, and reliability really matter.

The course was for sure challenging (in a fun way), especially while leading a team with high-impact projects at work, but the staff were incredibly supportive and accommodating throughout. A huge thanks to Prof. Lei Li and the course staff for an excellent (and very humbling) class. Also the guest lecturers! :)

If you’re working on LLM infrastructure or systems ↔ ML, I’d love to connect!

post.content:
"I was drowning."

That's how Amir Klein describes his first month as a PM at monday.com, tasked with building their first AI agent.

Context about the project lived everywhere: Slack channels, Notion pages, Monday boards, decks, Google Docs. Hundreds of fragments of context he couldn't keep straight.

Instead of doing what most of us do—trying to hold it all in his head—he tried something different.

He dumped everything into a ChatGPT Project. Word vomited everything on his mind. Even asked it for help on how to get started.

"Finally, I felt like I could smell a roadmap on the horizon, a direction was forming, and things began to click."

In this week's newsletter, Amir shares the exact system he used to build his PM second brain with ChatGPT—and how it helped him go from overwhelmed to shipping a major AI product launch.

(The workflow works in Claude and Gemini too, he shows how to set it up in all three)

Check it out: https://lnkd.in/gm2VWDmn

Our customers want applicant mis-representations caught upfront and fraud flagged instantly. This allows them to keep friction down for dealers, straight-through process more loans, and significantly reduce credit losses.

What’s next: This partnership with Invictus allows us to double down on our mission: bridging intelligence across financial institutions to mitigate the $50B in losses our industry experienced in the last 12 months while freeing up back-office staff. actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
As we've worked with countless AI teams to build, test and deploy agents, we've noticed an interesting trend: 𝐃𝐚𝐭𝐚 𝐬𝐜𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐬𝐨𝐟𝐭𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐞𝐧𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐦𝐞𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐠.

LLM-based features are built as software and tested like ML systems. There's a new workflow required to create and maintain the high-accuracy evals needed to test and govern these systems at scale.

We're calling it eval engineering.

Most teams can't trust their agents because they can't trust their evals. Industry research shows LLM-as-a-judge evals achieve only 70% accuracy, at best. 

Eval engineering solves this through a disciplined lifecycle:

1️⃣ Start with an LLM-as-a-judge prototype
2️⃣ Tune the eval with domain expertise to achieve 95% accuracy
3️⃣ Fine-tune a small language model eval for production-scale observability 
4️⃣ Deploy as real-time guardrails that adapt to drift, and stop failures before they reach users

The results speak for themselves: 

→ A Fortune 50 telecommunications company scaled from 1 agent to 47 agents in 8 months 

→ A Fortune 50 CPG business went from POC to production 15x faster and was able to observe 100% of their traffic 

Watch our CEO Vikram Chatterji explain the three core principles of eval engineering and why 2026 will be the year of trustworthy agents 👇

Plus, if you want to learn how to practice eval engineering yourself, we're launching a free 5-part course covering everything from eval creation to production guardrails. Register for the lessons below.

actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
We did a "Cursor for Product Managers" session today with 1,000+ people who joined! 

Our mission with Cursor is to help our customers build an AI-powered engineering team that can be an order of magnitude more productive.

This means enabling value beyond just engineers. For our most frontier customers, 10-20% of their Cursor users are not engineers - PMs, Designers, Data Scientists, Technical Sales, and more.

For PMs specifically, here are some of the use-cases I've found useful:
- Asking questions about the code base without needing to rely on engineers
- Cloud Agents to do small product tasks and feature requests
- Prototyping with a dummy version of your app for new features and ideas
- Bugbot to reliably ship code without bogging down engineering with review
- Make entire implementation plans with Cursor and add to ticket to make handoff simple
- Use Cursor + Linear/Jira MCPs to manage and prune your backlog directly from Cursor
- Debugging production issues with the Datadog MCP 

Shoutout Emily Gavrilenko and Zoë Barnswell for organizing this webinar!

actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
2026 is poised to be a pivotal year as the world begins to pursue Behavioral General Intelligence (BGI). We see inflection in this research area in 2025.

Currently, I see three branches of AI goals:

1. 🧠 AGI (Artificial General Intelligence): The pursuit of solving complex tasks that exceed human capabilities.
2. 🌏 World Models: The aim to develop AI that understands the physics of the world and can interact with it effectively.
3. 👥 BGI (Behavioral General Intelligence): The focus on understanding and simulating human behavior. 

While AGI and World Models have significant mainstream attention, BGI and human behavior simulation have yet to receive the same level of visibility.

Each of these goals requires fundamentally different approaches. Vectorial AI  has taken on the ambitious challenge of cracking BGI.

repost.content:
✨ 🐒 Introducing Our First AI System - Sapiens 1.0: State-of-the-Art Creating Synthetic Users when benchmarked against recent behavior foundation models and standard LLMs.

At Vectorial AI, we're pushing the boundaries to create high-quality synthetic users

🏁  "Sapiens 1.0" — Is a System that learns from large corpus of behavioral data to create high-fidelity synthetic users. Our Algorithm is inspired by matrix factorization and TextGrad . It updates the characteristics of the synthetics users until the delta between synthetic opinion and real opinions reduces. DM me for white paper access. 

Benchmarking process: 
We benchmarked on Amazon reviews dataset (1M+ reviews), where Sapiens achieved SOTA performance in predicting human opinions based on product descriptions as stimulus, outperforming behavior foundation models (like Be.FM and Centaur) and standard LLMs.

We've joined Berkeley SkyDeck (University of California, Berkeley) academic ecosystem to accelerate our research and to bring our product to market. Inspired by amazing research from Berkeley AI Research (https://lnkd.in/e7PCA-9X) and Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI) in human behavior simulation. I am excited to be back in academia, continuing the research I started at Carnegie Mellon University HCI and LTI departments.

Our Vision: Use Sapiens to create the entire human population in synthetic form.

actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
✨ 🐒 Introducing Our First AI System - Sapiens 1.0: State-of-the-Art Creating Synthetic Users when benchmarked against recent behavior foundation models and standard LLMs.

At Vectorial AI, we're pushing the boundaries to create high-quality synthetic users

🏁  "Sapiens 1.0" — Is a System that learns from large corpus of behavioral data to create high-fidelity synthetic users. Our Algorithm is inspired by matrix factorization and TextGrad . It updates the characteristics of the synthetics users until the delta between synthetic opinion and real opinions reduces. DM me for white paper access. 

Benchmarking process: 
We benchmarked on Amazon reviews dataset (1M+ reviews), where Sapiens achieved SOTA performance in predicting human opinions based on product descriptions as stimulus, outperforming behavior foundation models (like Be.FM and Centaur) and standard LLMs.

We've joined Berkeley SkyDeck (University of California, Berkeley) academic ecosystem to accelerate our research and to bring our product to market. Inspired by amazing research from Berkeley AI Research (https://lnkd.in/e7PCA-9X) and Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI) in human behavior simulation. I am excited to be back in academia, continuing the research I started at Carnegie Mellon University HCI and LTI departments.

Our Vision: Use Sapiens to create the entire human population in synthetic form.

Three Ways to Get Involved with us:
🔬 AI Researchers → Collaborate on building the world's largest synthetic population and publishing research 
⚙️ We are hiring!! engineers. → Join us as a Founding MLOps Engineer , Full stack developer to help support infrastructure and build out different usecases of simulations with synthetic users.
🚀 Product Leaders who want to be customers are looking to use synthetic users to run simulations to "Build right product 10x Faster"

📅 Book time: https://lnkd.in/eFJM97Ve

Coming soon: Research publication on Sapiens 1.0 + A Podcast on how we started 
#AIResearch #HumanBehaviorSimulation #SOTA #SyntheticUsers #ProductDevelopementSimulation

Special thanks to our amazing advisors who are helping us bring our tech to market.
Laura Moon, Arun Balaraman, Alex Mermod
Chon Tang, Sanjay Velappan, Caroline Winnett, Brian Bordley, 
---
Amol Sharma,  Andreas Lehmann, Anindita Sampath, Ipsita Basu, Praveen Joseph, CFA, Rajat Verma, Priyanshi Shrivastava

By combining agents with these behavioral foundation models and high-quality datasets, we are creating synthetic users that deliver on the promise of simulation engine for software industry.

actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
🚀 2025 has seen significant advancements in fundamental research on capturing user behavior/personas with AI. This is a turning point for product development simulations. This is the future we’re building towards at Vectorial AI. Imagine getting feedback on your product is as fast as vibe-coding them. In hours, you can have thousands of synthetic users 🤖  guide you on your next product iteration.  #vibexperimentation

⌛ In 2025, foundational research—such as Be .FM and StanfordHAI’s —has shown a new breed of foundation models and agents that can capture user behavior — opening possibilities for simulation of product development.

🏁 For decades, industries like semiconductors and aerospace have leapt forward with simulations. They could test, iterate, and perfect before building in the real world.  Vectorial AI is building Simulation Engine for Software Industry.

#Staytuned for our Benchmarking results on Simulation with Synthetic Users.
#VectorialAI #vibeexperimentation

actor.name: Sachin Kumar
actor.position: Senior Data Scientist III at LexisNexis | Experienced Agentic AI and Generative AI Expert

post.content:
Excited to announce about my first US Patent(US 11,694,033 B2) got granted and published just last month. As part of this patent, me and my former colleagues at LexisNexis, created an Iterative multi-concept search methodology with continuous feedback by users engaging with legal concepts presented.
Another interesting thing about this patent's idea, was that we came up with this idea and implementation during a hackathon at LexisNexis, which we also won. Huge congrats to my fellow team members Dhruv Sakalley , Kate Farmer, Ankur Oberai, Michael Etgen, Sanket Shukl ,with whom i got the great opportunity to collaborate on this idea.

actor.name: Anirudh Mani
actor.position: Building creative AI tools for Artists

post.content:
A long-overdue update 🙂 

I've had such a wonderful journey at Amazon Alexa, where I was fortunate to work alongside some exceptionally talented peers and mentors. As a Scientist, I had the opportunity to learn and grow while tackling some really challenging and largely unexplored research problems. At the same time, it was rewarding to see our ideas come to life through deploying our solutions at a massive scale. I'm so very grateful for the experiences at Amazon Alexa that have prepared me for this next exciting chapter of my life ...

I'm happy to share that I'm returning to my music tech roots with Lemonaide Music where I'll be researching and building creative AI tools for musicians 🎵 ✨  As a producer myself, I've always tinkered with and built AI tools for my own creative process. Now, I have the opportunity to do it for other artists, with other artists! I'm excited and grateful, and can't wait to share more updates here going forward!

actor.name: Hardik Nahata
actor.position: Senior ML Engineer @ PayPal | Building Scalable AI Agents | Mentoring ML Talent | Sharing AI & ML Insights

post.content:
🎉 Exciting Announcement! 🎉

I am absolutely thrilled to share that I have embarked on a new chapter in my career. I've started working Full-Time as a Data Scientist at Chegg Inc., in the heart of Silicon Valley, California! 🇺🇸 Words cannot express how grateful and honored I am to be part of such an innovative and forward-thinking company 🚀

At Chegg Inc., I am diving headfirst into the captivating world of Large Language Models #LLMs, #PromptEngineering, and cutting-edge AI. The opportunity to work on groundbreaking projects and contribute to the development of innovative solutions is incredibly exciting! 💡

I want to extend my gratitude to my parents, friends, and interviewers: Victoria Suarez, Sachin Kumar, Jonathan Ballard, Sakshi Bhargava, and Priya Venkat, PhD for recognizing my skills and potential for this role. Their belief in me and the opportunity they have given me to be a part of the exceptionally skilled Data Science Team is truly humbling. ✨

I would also like to express my appreciation to my recruiter Magdalena Strzyzewska. She went above and beyond, coordinating and aligning the schedules of both myself and the interviewers, spanning across the Eastern and Pacific time zones, ensuring a smooth and successful interview process. Special thanks to Taranveer Singh for onboarding me and quickly getting me up to speed! ✨

In addition to my new position, I am excited to announce the launch of my topmate.io page! 🌟 As a firm believer in the power of community and mentorship, I am eager to give back and support others in their careers. Through my Topmate page, I am offering a range of valuable services to aspiring students, graduates, and professionals. Together, let's unlock your potential and pave the way for success! 🚀 (Link in first comment and bio)

Thank you to everyone who has been a part of my journey thus far. Your support, encouragement, and belief in my abilities have been instrumental in my success. I am beyond excited for what lies ahead and can't wait to make a positive impact in Artificial Intelligence and beyond. ✨

#newbeginnings  #datascientist  #lifeatchegg  #ai #mentorship #siliconvalley #machinelearning  #artificialintelligence #datascience

actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
💡 I collaborated with healthcare expert Jasmine Kaur to build an AI agent for a real-world use case in the life sciences industry, specifically for sales strategy planning. Together, we examined sales leaders’ workflows, pinpointed implementation bottlenecks, and designed the overall architecture. Our primary goal was simple: to minimize workflow disruptions and reduce fragmented information silos.

⚒️ I applied my skills in Agent building and Gen. AI software development by:
• Selecting the optimal vector database stack
• Refining data-ingestion pipelines, and
• Integrating agentic UX principles for more intuitive interactions.

🚀 With that, I’m thrilled to introduce the COAS Labs Pharma Sales Strategy Copilot, a Chrome Extension I developed using state-of-the-art AI Agent frameworks to transform how pharma teams craft their sales strategy. By unifying insights from #Veeva, #IQVIA, and other enterprise sources into a single interface, the Copilot provides context-aware suggestions and actionable insights, streamlining monthly and quarterly planning.

What’s next? Continuing to enhance the UX and the Agent's capabilities.

#AIAgents #Agents #salesstrategy #pharma #AI #pharmasales #extension

author.name: Harvinder Laliya
author.info: Creating AI agents and agentic user experiences

postedAt.postedAgoText: 1 year ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

repost.content:
💹  Writing Pharma Sales Strategy Reports with AI: Building an #AIagent powered Chrome Extension . This tool was built with LangChain’s langgraph agent building platform developed by the incredible Harrison Chase and team. 

When I launched new products, preparing #salesstrategy involved surfacing the right insights from #Veeva (CRM), sales rep calls and interviews, #IQVIA reports etc. and it takes lot of time to make consensus. 
...
This tool digs the gold from multiple enterprise integrations. I collaborated with Harvinder Singh Laliya as part of CAOS Labs on engineering this solution to help iterate this experiment at record speed.
 ...  
Most AI systems are not integrated with enterprise data; I wanted an in-document 📚 solution for faster, more natural collaboration.  I welcome your feedback — this is an experimental project as I dive into developing use cases for practical tasks and the pharma 💊  industry.
...
For my friends leading sales strategy at Pharma/Biotech companies, I’d love to help you prepare the best versions of your monthly and quarterly sales insights and strategy planning.
...
What It Does:
📞 User Insights: Surfaces insights from Veeva, customer interviews, analytics reports.
📑 Writing Suggestions: Factors in product context and organizational data.
🔎 Context Summary: Pulls info from multiple sources (e.g., CRM dashboards, field presentations).
📊 Document Stats: Provides readability metrics for a cleaner final output.
...
Check Out:
📹 Demo Video to see it in action.
🧮 Architecture Diagram for a peek under the hood.
#AIagents #learnbydoing #salesstrategy #pharma #pharmasales #experiments #langgraph

article.title: Event Registration

article.linkLabel: Open article: Event Registration by cs.cmu.edu

commentary:
This is on point ..As you mentioned "Amazon's of the world have it solved" but the mid-tier don't. There is also a fundamental challenge for them because the mid-tier companies lack external data and insights and they don't experiment and change their pricing dynamically to come to a price that would maximize their revenue. Amzaon's of the world run these AB tests regularly.

The mid tier companies  might also benefit from insights on what can be small changes they can make to increase the value for money for customers or their perception

If we can break down products into their attributes as you said that's gold mine and you can find competitor products that match these attributes so that you can get to fair value of the product at which it will sell well.

Are you guys targeting e-commerce or brick n mortor or both?   because when it comes to democratic platform like amazon to sell your product, There is also the factor of how do you maximize the exposure of the product when it comes to recommendation and SEO . which is a different beast all together

post.content:
So, finally after 4 months I completed the Udacity's Sensor Fusion Nanodegree Program. In this course I learned the concept of Lidar, Radar and camera sensors and most importantly fusing them using Unscented kalman Filter to get the best update. 
I worked on 4 different real world projects in this course through which I got a much better understanding on this topic, which lays a foundation for Self driving cars. 
I am very thankful to Udacity and all my peer groups in the course. It was fun learning with you!!
#udacity #nanodegree #sensorfusion #selfdrivingcars

actor.name: Taranveer Singh
actor.position: Founder @ Vectorial AI - Building Sapiens with AI

post.content:
This is only Indian companies who discriminate against Aging people. With the life expectancy increasing and people becoming more aware of their health. 

It should be encouraged to have diversity and inclusion in companies in India. people put an age bar right from age of 45.  It is actually the time when people can contribute more to the organizations. An organization needs both experience and energy. Younger folks cannot channelize their energies in the most productive manner without experienced hands to mentor. People could take senior people on contractual assignments and get their lifetime experience on the plate and treat it as an experiential wealth of their organization. Knowledge & experience is critical to the success of the business in a competitive market. The elderly could also be engaged as advisors with fixed retainership and companies stand to gain to get more out of these engagements. Wastage of experience and knowledge is a national loss.

repost.content:
I've always struggled to understand the concept of retirement, why would someone stop work just because they have reached a certain age. 

Historically, it seems to be product of industrial age/revolution.

Is that a relevant idea today (in white collar jobs)?

post.content:
Current openings at Rucha Engineering R&D Center:
Robotics /Embedded Systems Engineer:
Requirements:
1.A completed academic M.S. /M. Tech degree in Mechanical/Electronics/Electrical/Computer Science from a premier institute.
2. Expert programming skills and knowledge of C/C++, Objective-C, MATLAB
3. Development experience on Linux, Android, iOS and OS X.
Willing to make a long term commitment of 2-3years.
4.  Work experience: 0-4 years
5. Interest in microcontrollers, PLC, sensors, wireless communication, Computer Vision
6.  Familiarity with path planning, motion planning, perception, vision systems, localization, mapping, control systems
7.  Familiarity with ROS (Robot Operating System).
8. Excellent software design, problem solving and debugging skills
9. Any prior experience of working with PIC, ARM, Atmel, Atmega processors
10. Expert knowledge/experience of working with PLC’s.
11. Organised working, good and thorough documentation habits.
12. Strong verbal and written communication and teamwork skills
13. Strong preference given to the candidates completing MOOC courses in 
14. Computer Vision/Robot Autonomy/Machine Learning/Manipulation and Mobility Control (completion certificate needed).

Interested applicants: Kindly email your profiles on recruitment@ruchagroup.com
